steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence says Chicago's public art can 'satisfy any art lover.' The passage supports this claim by listing art ranging from monumental sculptures to innovative street art — emphasizing variety. The blank must name the quality of Chicago's public art that makes universal satisfaction possible.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — breadth of: means range or variety — exactly what the passage demonstrates with its examples spanning sculptures to murals, and exactly what enables the collection to appeal to all tastes. ✓
  • Choice B — controversy over: introduces conflict, which contradicts the passage's positive framing of the collection as 'outstanding.' ✗
  • Choice C — confusion about: implies the art is disorienting or unclear — opposite of what would satisfy any art lover. ✗
  • Choice D — apathy toward: means indifference, which directly contradicts the idea that the art satisfies lovers of it. ✗
That matches Choice A — breadth of.
